title: D1.3.16 Report on evaluation of analytical methods according with compilers needs (TG1)
author: INSA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document describes the guidelines to evaluate method of analysis used in determination of nutrient values described in scientific literature or in laboratory reports. The guidance document was produced in order to support the introduction of quality management systems into food composition activities. EuroFIR analytical method guidelines provides data compilers with comprehensive information for each nutrient: acceptable methods, e.g. approval by standardization organisation (AOAC, European committee for standardization (CEN), Nordic committee on food analysis (NMKL), etc.), applicable food matrices, and expected precision and concentration range validity. Guidance is given to help the data compiler to evaluate method performance, i.e. accuracy, reproducibility, and traceability. Furthermore, the guidelines provide compilers with flexible tools to evaluate the quality of data obtained by compatible in-house methods that might include modifications in key method steps, e.g. extraction, separation, detection, quantification.
